meaning: adj.快活的,兴致好的v.有精神,(使)高兴
examples: Chirk up, your troubles will soon be over.
I really hope he'll chirk up, and get along comfortable.
It would be a tragedy if the chirk and the crick of this once common creature should no more be heard as a choral accompaniment to spring.
More than 300 years later they were discovered in Chirk Castle before being sold at auction.
Minor injuries units at Chirk, Colwyn Bay and Ruthin will also close.
